title: Pier Seven Argument
summary: Dock workers reported an argument near pier seven on a foggy night. A tall figure matching Reeves was seen with a shorter man wearing a cap. The warehouse door slammed on the quay shortly after.

title: Net Mender's Account
summary: A fisherman mending nets by lamp-light saw two figures on the waterfront. Fog was thick and faces were hard to make out. The word warehouse was shouted before footsteps faded toward the jetty.

title: Anchor Pub Alibi
summary: Witness claims Reeves was drinking at the Anchor pub until closing. Dock workers had already gone home. The warehouse clock was reportedly stopped that week.

title: Margaret Hayes Sighting
summary: A woman saw a man near the docks on Tuesday evening. She believed it was Reeves accompanied by another man. She was told to keep quiet about CASE-42.

title: Empty Docks Report
summary: The docks were empty that night according to one witness. Footsteps echoed on the cobbles and someone shouted Reeves name. Then silence fell over the quay.

title: Warehouse Midnight Shift
summary: A warehouse clerk insisted she worked until midnight. Dock workers had left hours earlier. She denied Reeves was on the premises and pointed investigators toward the pub.

title: Ship Arrival Log
summary: A cargo ship arrived through heavy fog on the night in question. Pier hands unloaded crates until dawn. Several men argued on the quay about missing freight from the warehouse.

title: Accountant's Satchel
summary: The company accountant left the office at six carrying a leather satchel. A figure followed him down River Lane toward the station. Ledger pages were later found scattered near the platform.

title: Station Platform Watch
summary: A clerk saw the numbers man near the station looking nervous. CASE-17 paperwork was tucked under his arm. He boarded the evening train without checking his ticket.

title: Office Ledger Gap
summary: Internal audit found a gap in the office ledger spanning three months. The accountant had access to every account code. Cash deposits at the station branch stopped matching the books.

title: Warehouse Inventory Check
summary: Nothing unusual was reported at the warehouse that week. Stock counts matched shipping manifests. The fraud appeared confined to the downtown office.

title: River Lane Tail
summary: A typist leaving late saw two men arguing about invoices on River Lane. One carried a briefcase of receipts. They turned toward the station when a tram passed.

title: Bank Deposit Anomaly
summary: The branch manager noticed duplicate deposit slips filed under different account numbers. The accountant signed both batches. CASE-17 was flagged after a customer complained about missing funds.

title: Receipt Burner
summary: Janitorial staff found ashes behind the office boiler. Partial receipts were still legible. Investigators linked the scraps to ledger entries removed in February.

title: Black Motorcar Stop
summary: A black motorcar without plates stopped outside a shop on Mill Road. Two men got out and one wore a grey overcoat. A third man stayed in the car watching the street.

title: Overcoat Return
summary: The grey overcoat man returned on Thursday asking for directions to the docks. CASE-88 witnesses disagree on whether he carried a package. Shop staff remember his polished shoes.

title: Plateless Vehicle
summary: A motorcar with no registration plates was seen circling the block twice. Neighbours noted the same grey overcoat at the window. The vehicle left toward the river road at dusk.

title: Surveillance on Mill Road
summary: Residents reported a parked motorcar outside three homes on Mill Road. The driver never entered any building. Grey overcoat was seen consulting a folded map.

title: Tail to the Docks
summary: A shopkeeper told police the overcoat man asked for the fastest route to the waterfront. He paid in crisp notes and left no name. The motorcar turned south minutes later.

title: Chief's Frustration
summary: CASE-88 was described as a mess by a longtime constable. Multiple witnesses mention the same grey overcoat but disagree on dates. The motorcar sightings cluster around early April.

title: Neighbourhood Noise Complaint
summary: A family on Orchard Street complained about shouting next door. The neighbour works nights and keeps odd hours. No physical evidence was found during the first visit.

title: Shop Window Reflection
summary: A baker saw a couple arguing in the reflection of her shop window. They moved toward the tram stop on High Street. She knew them as quiet tenants from the flat above.

title: Garden Gate Witness
summary: An elderly man watering his garden heard a door slam two houses away. He recognised the voice of the woman who runs the corner shop. She later denied being home that afternoon.

title: Corner Shop Alibi
summary: The shopkeeper said she closed early for inventory. Neighbours claim the lights were on until midnight. A delivery boy recalls knocking with no answer.

title: Tram Stop Argument
summary: Passengers at the High Street tram stop reported a heated exchange between two neighbours. One threatened to involve the landlord. Both gave conflicting accounts to the constable.

title: Landlord's Ledger
summary: The landlord produced rent receipts for every tenant on Orchard Street. One flat was paid in cash weekly. He noted frequent late-night visitors to that address.

title: Midnight Radio
summary: A teenager heard a radio playing loudly from the flat above the shop. The music stopped when police knocked. The tenant claimed she was visiting family across town.
